source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm LXXI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In all the holy Scriptures the grace of God that delivereth us commendeth itself to us, in order that it may have us commended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is sung of in this Psalm, whereof we have undertaken to speak.…This grace the Apostle commendeth: by this he got to have the Jews for enemies, boasting of the letter of the law and of their own justice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then commending in the lesson which hath been read, he saith thus: “For I am the least of the Apostles, that am not worthy to be called an Apostle, because I persecuted the Church of God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But therefore mercy,” he saith, “I obtained, because ignorant I did it in unbelief.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then a little afterwards, “Faithful the saying is, and worthy of all acceptation, that Christ Jesus came into the world to save sinners, of whom I am first.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Were there before him not any sinners?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then, was he the first then?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, going before all men not in time, but in evil disposition.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But therefore,” he saith, “mercy I obtained,” in order that in me Christ Jesus might show all long-suffering, for the imitation of those that shall believe in Him unto life eternal: that is, every sinner and unjust man, already despairing of himself, already having the mind of a gladiator, so as to do whatsoever he willeth, because he must needs be condemned, may yet observe the Apostle Paul, to whom so great cruelty and so very evil a disposition was forgiven by God; and by not despairing of himself may he be turned unto God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This grace God doth commend to us in this Psalm also.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title then of this Psalm is, as usual, a title intimating on the threshold what is being done in the house: “To David himself for the sons of Jonadab, and for those that were first led captive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Jonadab (he is commended to us in the prophecy of Jeremiah) was a certain man, who had enjoined his sons not to drink wine, and not to dwell in houses, but in tents.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the commandment of the father the sons kept and observed, and by this earned a blessing from the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now the Lord had not commanded this, but their own father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But they so received it as though it were a commandment from the Lord their God; for even though the Lord had not commanded that they should drink no wine and should dwell in tents; yet the Lord had commanded that sons should obey their father.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In this case alone a son ought not to obey his father, if his father should have commanded anything contrary to the Lord his God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For indeed the father ought not to be angry, when God is preferred before him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when a father doth command that which is not contrary to God; he must be heard as God is: because to obey one’s father God hath enjoined.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God then blessed the sons of Jonadab because of their obedience, and thrust them in the teeth of His disobedient people, reproaching them, because while the sons of Jonadab were obedient to their father, they obeyed not their God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But while Jeremiah was treating of these topics, he had this object in regard to the people of Israel, that they should prepare themselves to be led for captivity into Babylon, and should not hope for any other thing, but that they were to be captives.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title then of this Psalm seemeth from thence to have taken its hue, so that when he had said, “Of the sons of Jonadab;” he added, “and of them that were first led captive:” not that the sons of Jonadab were led captive, but because to them that were to be led captive there were opposed the sons of Jonadab, because they were obedient to their father: in order that they might understand that they had been made captive, because they were not obedient to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is added also that Jonadab is interpreted, “the Lord’s spontaneous one.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this, the Lord’s spontaneous one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Serving God freely with the will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, the Lord’s spontaneous one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In me are, O God, Thy vows, which I will render of praise to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, the Lord’s spontaneous one?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Voluntarily I will sacrifice to Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if the Apostolic teaching admonisheth a slave to serve a human master, not as though of necessity, but of good will, and by freely serving make himself in heart free; how much more must God be served with whole and full and free will, who seeth thy very will?…The first man made us captive, the second man hath delivered us from captivity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For as in Adam all die, so also in Christ all shall be made alive.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in Adam they die through the flesh’s nativity, in Christ they are delivered through the heart’s faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It was not in thy power not to be born of Adam: it is in thy power to believe in Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Howsoever much then thou shalt have willed to belong to the first man, unto captivity thou wilt belong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is, shalt have willed to belong? or what is, shalt belong?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already thou belongest: cry out, “Who shall deliver me from the body of this death?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us hear then this man crying out this.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, in Thee I have hoped, O Lord, I shall not be confounded for everlasting” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already I have been confounded, but not for everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For how is he not confounded, to whom is said, “What fruit had ye in these things wherein ye now blush?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then shall be done, that we may not be confounded for everlasting?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Draw near unto Him, and be ye enlightened, and your faces shall not blush.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confounded ye are in Adam, withdraw from Adam, draw near unto Christ, and then ye shall not be confounded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thee I have hoped, O Lord, I shall not be confounded for everlasting.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If in myself I am now confounded, in Thee I shall not be confounded for everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thine own righteousness deliver me, and save me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not in mine own, but in Thine own: for if in mine own, I shall be one of those whereof he saith, “Being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and their own righteousness willing to establish, to the righteousness of God they were not made subject.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, “in Thine own righteousness,” not in mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For mine is what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Iniquity hath gone before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when I shall be righteous, Thine own righteousness it will be: for by righteousness given to me by Thee I shall be righteous; and it shall be so mine, as that it be Thine, that is, given to me by Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I believe on Him that justifieth an ungodly man, so that my faith is counted for righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even so then the righteousness shall be mine, not however as though mine own, not as though by mine own self given to myself: as they thought who through the letter made their boast, and rejected grace.…It is a small thing then that thou acknowledge the good thing which is in thee to be from God, unless also on that account thou exalt not thyself above him that hath not yet, who perchance when he shall have received, will outstrip thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when Saul was a stoner of Stephen, how many were the Christians of whom he was persecutor!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, when he was converted, all that had gone before he surpassed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore say thou to God that which thou hearest in the Psalm, “In Thee I have hoped, O Lord, I shall not be confounded for everlasting: in Thine own righteousness,” not in mine, “deliver me, and save me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Incline unto me Thine ear.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This also is a confession of humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that saith, “Incline unto me,” is confessing that he is lying like a sick man laid at the feet of the Physician standing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, observe that it is a sick man that is speaking: “Incline unto me Thine ear, and save me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be Thou unto me for a protecting God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the darts of the enemy reach unto me: for I am not able to protect myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And a small thing is “protecting:” he hath added, “and for a walled place, that Thou mayest save me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For a walled place” be Thou to me, be Thou my walled place.…Behold, God Himself hath become the place of thy fleeing unto, who at first was the fearful object of thy fleeing from.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For a walled place,” he saith, be Thou to me, “that Thou mayest save me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I shall not be safe except in Thee: except Thou shalt have been my rest, my sickness shall not be able to be made whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lift me from the earth; upon Thee I will lie, in order that I may rise unto a walled place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What can be better walled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When unto that place thou shalt have fled for refuge, tell me what adversaries thou wilt dread?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who will lie in wait, and come at thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A certain man is said from the summit of a mountain to have cried out, when an Emperor was passing by, “I speak not of thee:” the other is said to have looked back and to have said, “Nor I of thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He had despised an Emperor with glittering arms, with mighty army.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From whence?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From a strong place.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If he was secure on a high spot of earth, how secure art thou on Him by whom heaven and earth were made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I, if for myself I shall have chosen another place, shall not be able to be safe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Choose thou indeed, O man, if thou shalt have found one, a place better walled.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is not then a place whither to flee from Him, except we flee to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou wilt escape Him angry, flee to Him appeased.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my firmament and my refuge Thou art.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My firmament” is what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Through Thee I am firm, and by Thee I am firm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For my firmament and my refuge Thou art:” in order that I may be made firm by Thee, in whatever respects I shall have been made infirm in myself, I will flee for refuge unto Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For firm the grace of Christ maketh thee, and immovable against all temptations of the enemy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there is there too human frailness, there is there still the first captivity, there is there too the law in the members fighting against the law of the mind, and willing to lead captive in the law of sin: still the body which is corrupt presseth down the soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Howsoever firm thou be by the grace of God, so long as thou still bearest an earthly vessel, wherein the treasure of God is, something must be dreaded even from that same vessel of clay.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “my firmament Thou art,” in order that I may be firm in this world against all temptations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if many they are, and they trouble me: “my refuge Thou art.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I will confess mine infirmity, to the end that I may be timid like a “hare,” because I am full of thorns like a “hedgehog.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And as in another Psalm is said, “The rock is a refuge for the hedgehogs and the hares:” but the Rock was Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, deliver me from the hand of the sinner” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Generally, sinners, among whom is toiling he that is now to be delivered from captivity: he that now crieth, “Unhappy man I, who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The grace of God through Jesus Christ our Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Within is a foe, that law in the members; there are without also enemies: unto what cryest thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto Him, to whom hath been cried, “From my secret sins cleanse me, O Lord, and from strange sins spare Thy servant.”…But these sinners are of two kinds: there are some that have received Law, there are others that have not received: all the heathen have not received Law, all Jews and Christians have received Law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the general term is sinner; either a transgressor of the Law, if he hath received Law; or only unjust without Law, if he hath not received the Law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of both kinds speaketh the Apostle, and saith, “They that without Law have sinned, without Law shall perish, and they that in the Law have sinned, by the Law shall be judged.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou that amid both kinds dost groan, say to God that which thou hearest in the Psalm, “My God, deliver me from the hand of the sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what sinner?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the hand of him that transgresseth the Law, and of the unjust man.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He that transgresseth the Law is indeed also unjust; for not unjust he is not, that transgresseth the Law: but every one that transgresseth the Law is unjust, not every unjust man doth transgress the Law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Where there is not a Law,” saith the Apostle, “neither is there transgression.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then that have not received Law, may be called unjust, transgressors they cannot be called.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Both are judged after their deservings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I that from captivity will to be delivered through Thy grace, cry to Thee, “Deliver me from the hand of the sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, from the hand of him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the power of him, that while he is raging, he lead me not unto consenting with him; that while he lieth in wait, he persuade not to iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From the hand of the sinner and of the unjust man.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, there followeth the reason why I say this: “for Thou art my patience” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now if He is patience rightly, He is that also which followeth, “O Lord, my hope from my youth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My patience, because my hope: or rather my hope, because my patience.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Tribulation,” saith the Apostle, “worketh patience, patience probation, but probation hope, but hope confoundeth not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason in Thee I have hoped, O Lord, I shall not be confounded for everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, my hope from my youth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From thy youth is God thy hope?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is He not also from thy boyhood, and from thine infancy?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly, saith he.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see what followeth, that thou mayest not think that I have said this, “my hope from my youth,” as if God noways profited mine infancy or my boyhood; hear what followeth: “In Thee I have been strengthened from the womb.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear yet: “From the belly of my mother Thou art my Protector” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why then, “from my youth,” except it was the period from which I began to hope in Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For before in Thee I was not hoping, though Thou wast my Protector, that didst lead me safe unto the time, when I learned to hope in Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But from my youth I began in Thee to hope, from the time when Thou didst arm me against the Devil, so that in the girding of Thy host being armed with Thy faith, love, hope, and the rest of Thy gifts, I waged conflict against Thine invisible enemies, and heard from the Apostle, “There is not for us a wrestling against flesh and blood, but against principalities, and powers,” etc. There a young man it is that doth fight against these things: but though he be a young man, he falleth, unless He be the hope of Him to whom he crieth, “O Lord, my hope from my youth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In Thee is my singing alway.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is it only from the time when I began to hope in Thee until now?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, but “alway.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “alway”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only in the time of faith, but also in the time of sight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For now, “So long as we are in the body we are absent from the Lord: for by faith we walk, not by sight:” there will be a time when we shall see that which being not seen we believe: but when that hath been seen which we believe, we shall rejoice: but when that hath been seen which they believed not, ungodly men shall be confounded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then will come the substance whereof there is now the hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But, “Hope which is seen is not hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if that which we see not we hope for, through patience we wait for it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then thou groanest, now unto a place of refuge thou runnest, in order that thou mayest be saved; now being in infirmity thou entreatest the Physician: what, when thou shalt have received perfect soundness also, what when thou shall have been made “equal to the Angels of God,” wilt thou then perchance forget that grace, whereby thou hast been delivered?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As it were a monster I have become unto many” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here in time of hope, in time of groaning, in time of humiliation, in time of sorrow, in time of infirmity, in time of the voice from the fetters—here then what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As it were a monster I have become unto many.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why, “As it were a monster”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do they insult me that think me a monster?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because I believe that which I see not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they being happy in those things which they see, exult in drink, in wantonness, in chamberings, in covetousness, in riches, in robberies, in secular dignities, in the whitening of a mud wall, in these things they exult: but I walk in a different way, contemning those things which are present, and fearing even the prosperous things of the world, and secure in no other thing but the promises of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they, “Let us eat and drink, for to-morrow we die.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What sayest thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Repeat it: “let us eat,” he saith, “and drink.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Come now, what hast thou said afterwards? “for to-morrow we die.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast terrified, not led me astray.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Certainly by the very thing which thou hast said afterwards, thou hast stricken me with fear to consent with thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For to-morrow we die,” thou hast said: and there hath preceded, “Let us eat and drink.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when thou hadst said, “Let us eat and drink;” thou didst add, “for to-morrow we die.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the other side from me, “Yea let us fast and pray, ‘for to-morrow we die.’”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I keeping this way, strait and narrow, “as it were a monster have become unto many: but Thou art a strong helper.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be Thou with me, O Lord Jesus, to say to me, faint not in the narrow way, I first have gone along it, I am the way itself, I lead, in Myself I lead, unto Myself I lead home.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore though “a monster I have become unto many;” nevertheless I will not fear, for “Thou art a strong Helper.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let my mouth be fulfilled with praise, that with hymn I may tell of Thy glory, all the day long Thy magnificence” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is “all the day long”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Without intermission.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In prosperity, because Thou dost comfort: in adversity, because Thou dost correct: before I was in being, because Thou didst make; when I was in being, because Thou didst give health: when I had sinned, because Thou didst forgive; when I was converted, because Thou didst help; when I had persevered, because Thou didst crown.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My hope from my youth, “cast me not away in time of old age” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this time of old age?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When my strength shall fail, forsake Thou not me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here God maketh this answer to thee, yea indeed let thy strength fail, in order that in thee mine may abide: in order that thou mayest say with the Apostle, “When I am made weak, then I am mighty.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not, that thou be cast away in that weakness, in that old age.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Was not thy Lord made weak on the Cross?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did not most mighty men and fat bulls before Him, as though a man of no strength, made captive and oppressed, shake the head and say, “If Son of God He is, let Him come down from the Cross”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Has he deserted because He was made weak, who preferred not to come down from the Cross, lest He should seem not to have displayed power, but to have yielded to them reviling?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What did He hanging teach thee, that would not come down, but patience amid men reviling, but that thou shouldest be strong in thy God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perchance too in His person was said, “As it were a monster I have become unto many, and Thou art a strong Helper.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In His person according to His weakness, not according to His power; according to that whereby He had transformed us into Himself, not according to that wherein He had Himself come down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He became a monster unto many.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And perchance the same was the old age of Him; because on account of its oldness it is not improperly called old age, and the Apostle saith, “Our old man hath been crucified together with Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If there was there our old man, old age was there; because old, old age.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nevertheless, because a true saying is, “Renewed as an eagle’s shall be Thy youth;” He rose Himself the third day, promised a resurrection at the end of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already there hath gone before the Head, the members are to follow.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why dost thou fear lest He should forsake thee, lest He cast thee away for the time of old age, when thy strength shall have failed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea at that time in thee will be the strength of Him, when thy strength shall have failed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do I say this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For mine enemies have spoken against me, and they that were keeping watch for My soul, have taken counsel together (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10): saying, God hath forsaken Him, persecute Him, and seize Him, for there is no one to deliver Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This hath been said concerning Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He that with the great power of Divinity, wherein He is equal to the Father, had raised to life dead persons, on a sudden in the hands of enemies became weak, and as if having no power, was seized.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When would He have been seized, except they had first said in their heart, “God hath forsaken Him?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence there was that voice on the Cross, “My God, My God, why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So then did God forsake Christ, though “God was in Christ reconciling the world to Himself,” though Christ was also God, out of the Jews indeed according to the flesh, “Who is over all things, God blessed for ever,”—did God forsake Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But in our old man our voice it was, because our old man was crucified together with Him: and of that same our old man He had taken a Body, because Mary was of Adam.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore the very thing which they thought, from the Cross He said, “Why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why do these men think Me left alone to their evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, think Me forsaken in their evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if they had known, the Lord of glory they had never crucified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Persecute and seize Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More familiarly however, brethren, let us take this of the members of Christ, and acknowledge our own voice in these words: because even He used such words in our person, not in His own power and majesty; but in that which He became for our sakes, not according to that which He was, who hath made us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, my God, be not far from me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So it is, and the Lord is not far off at all.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “The Lord is nigh unto them that have bruised the heart.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My God, unto my help look Thou.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be they confounded and fail that engage my soul” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hath he desired?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be they confounded and fail.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why hath he desired it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That engage my soul”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “That engage my soul”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Engaging as it were unto some quarrel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they are said to be engaged that are challenged to quarrel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then so it is, let us beware of men that engage our soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “That engage our soul”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: First provoking us to withstand God, in order that in our evil things God may displease us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when art thou right, so that to thee the God of Israel may be good, good to men right in heart?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When art thou right?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou hear?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When in that good which thou doest, God is pleasing to thee; but in that evil which thou sufferest, God is not displeasing to thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See ye what I have said, brethren, and be ye on your guard against men that engage your souls.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all men that deal with you in order to make you be wearied in sorrows and tribulations, have this aim, namely, that God may be displeasing to you in that which ye suffer, and there may go forth from your mouth, “What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what have I done?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then hast thou done nothing of evil, and art thou just, He unjust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A sinner I am, thou sayest, I confess, just I call not myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what, sinner, hast thou by any means done so much evil as he with whom it is well?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As much as Gaiuseius?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I know the evil doings of him, I know the iniquities of him, from which I, though a sinner, am very far; and yet I see him abounding in all good things, and I am suffering so great evil things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I do not then say, O God, “what have I done” to Thee, because I have done nothing at all of evil; but because I have not done so much as to deserve to suffer these things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, art thou just, He unjust?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wake up, wretched man, thy soul hath been engaged!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have not, he saith, called myself just.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then sayest thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A sinner I am, but I did not commit so great sins, as to deserve to suffer these things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou sayest not then to God, just I am, and Thou art unjust: but thou sayest, unjust I am, but Thou art more unjust.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold thy soul hath been engaged, behold now thy soul wageth war.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Against whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy soul, against God; that which hath been made against Him by whom it was made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even because thou art in being to cry out against Him, thou art ungrateful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Return, then, to the confession of thy sickness, and beg the healing hand of the Physician.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think thou not they are happy who flourish for a time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art being chastised, they are being spared: perchance for thee chastised and amended an inheritance is being kept in reserve.…Lastly, see what followeth, “Let them put on confusion and shame, that think evil things to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Confusion and shame,” confusion because of a bad conscience, shame because of modesty.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let this befall them, and they will be good.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I alway in Thee will hope, and will add to all Thy praise” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will add to all Thy praise,” ought to move us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More perfect wilt thou make the praise of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is there anything to be superadded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If already that is all praise, wilt thou add anything?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God was praised in all His good deeds, in every creature of His, in the whole establishment of all things, in the government and regulation of ages, in the order of seasons, in the height of Heaven, in the fruitfulness of the regions of earth, in the encircling of the sea, in every excellency of the creature everywhere brought forth, in the sons of men themselves, in the giving of the Law, in delivering His people from the captivity of the Egyptians, and all the rest of His wonderful works: not yet He had been praised for having raised up flesh unto life eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be there then this praise added by the Resurrection of our Lord Jesus Christ: in order that here we may perceive His voice above all past praise: thus it is that we rightly understand this also.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My mouth shall tell out Thy righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 15): not mine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From thence I will add to all Thy praise: because even that I am righteous, if righteous I am, is Thy righteousness in me, not mine own: for Thou dost justify the ungodly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the day long Thy salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “Thy salvation”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let no one assume to himself, that he saveth himself, “Of the Lord is Salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not any one by himself saveth himself, “Vain is man’s salvation.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the day long Thy Salvation:” at all times.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Something of adversity cometh, preach the Salvation of the Lord: something of prosperity cometh, preach the Salvation of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not preach in prosperity, and hold thy peace in adversity: otherwise there will not be that which hath been said, “all the day long.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all the day long is day together with its own night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do we when we say, for example, thirty days have gone by, mention the nights also; do we not under the very term days include the nights also?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Genesis what was said?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The evening was made, and the morning was made, one day.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore a whole day is the day together with its own night: for the night doth serve the day, not the day the night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whatever thou doest in mortal flesh, ought to serve righteousness: whatever thou doest by the commandment of God, be it not done for the sake of the advantage of the flesh, lest day serve night.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore all the day long speak of the praise of God, to wit, in prosperity and in adversity; in prosperity, as though in the day time; in adversity, as though in the night time: all the day long nevertheless speak of the praise of God, so that thou mayest not have sung to no purpose, “I will bless God at every time, alway the praise of Him is in my mouth.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore, he saith, “For I have not known tradings.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What are these tradings?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let traders hear and change their life; and if they have been such, be not such; let them not know what they have been, let them forget; lastly, let them not approve, not praise; let them disapprove, condemn, be changed, if trading is a sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For on this account, O thou trader, because of a certain eagerness for getting, whenever thou shalt have suffered loss, thou wilt blaspheme; and there will not be in thee that which hath been spoken of, “all the day long Thy praise.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But whenever for the price of the goods which thou art selling, thou not only liest, but even falsely swearest; how in thy mouth all the day long is there the praise of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While, if thou art a Christian, even out of thy mouth the name of God is being blasphemed, so that men say, see what sort of men are Christians!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore if this man for this reason speaketh the praise of God all the day long, because he hath not known tradings; let Christians amend themselves, let them not trade.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But a trader saith to me, behold I bring indeed from a distant quarter merchandise unto these places, wherein there are not those things which I have brought, by which means I may gain a living: I ask but as reward for my labour, that I may sell dearer than I have bought: for whence can I live, when it hath been written, “the worker is worthy of his reward”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he is treating of lying, of false swearing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This is the fault of me, not of trading: for I should not, if I would, be unable to do without this fault.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I then, the merchant, do not shift mine own fault to trading: but if I lie, it is I that lie, not the trade.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I might say, for so much I bought, but for so much I will sell; if thou pleasest, buy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the buyer hearing this truth would not be offended, and not a whit less all men would resort to me: because they would love truth more than gain.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of this then, he saith, admonish me, that I lie not, that I forswear not; not to relinquish business whereby I maintain myself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For to what dost thou put me when thou puttest me away from this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Perchance to some craft?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will be a shoemaker, I will make shoes for men.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not they too liars? are not they too false-swearers?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do they not, when they have contracted to make shoes for one man, when they have received money from another man, give up that which they were making, and undertake to make for another, and deceive him for whom they have promised to make speedily?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do they not often say, to-day I am about it, to-day I’ll get them done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Secondly, in the very sewing do they not commit as many frauds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are their doings and these are their sayings: but they are themselves evil, not the calling which they profess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All evil artificers, then, not fearing God, either for gain, or for fear of loss or want, do lie, do forswear themselves; there is no continual praise of God in them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then dost thou withdraw me from trading?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou that I be a farmer, and murmur against God thundering, so that, fearing hail, I consult a wizard, in order to learn what to do to protect me against the weather; so that I desire famine for the poor, in order that I may be able to sell what I have kept in store?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unto this dost thou bring me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But good farmers, thou sayest, do not such things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor do good traders do those things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why, even to have sons is an evil thing, for when their head is in pain, evil and unbelieving mothers seek for impious charms and incantations?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are the sins of men, not of things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A trader might thus speak to me—Look then, O Bishop, how thou understand the tradings which thou hast read in the Psalm: lest perchance thou understand not, and yet forbid me trading.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Admonish me then how I should live; if well, it shall be well with me: one thing however I know, that if I shall have been evil, it is not trading that maketh me so, but my iniquity.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whenever truth is spoken, there is nothing to be said against it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us inquire then what he hath called tradings, which indeed he that hath not known, all the day long doth praise God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Trading even in the Greek language is derived from action, and in the Latin from want of inaction: but whether it be from action or want of inaction, let us examine what it is.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they that are active traders, rely as it were upon their own action, they praise their works, they attain not to the grace of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore traders are opposed to that grace which this Psalm doth commend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it doth commend that grace, in order that no one may boast of his own works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because in a certain place is said, “Physicians shall not raise to life,” ought men to abandon medicine?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is this?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Under this name are understood proud men, promising salvation to men, whereas “of the Lord is Salvation.”…With reason the Lord drave from the Temple them to whom He said, “It is written, My House shall be called the House of prayer, but ye have made it a house of trading;” that is, boasting of your works, seeking no inaction, nor hearing the Scripture speaking against your unrest and trading, “be ye still, and see that I am the Lord.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there is in some copies, “For I have not known literature.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where some books have “trading,” there others “literature:” how they may accord is a hard matter to find out; and yet the discrepancy of interpreters perchance showeth the meaning, introduceth no error.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us inquire then how to understand literature also, lest we offend grammarians in the same way as we did traders a little before: because a grammarian too may live honourably in his calling, and neither forswear nor lie.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us examine then the literature which he hath not known, in whose mouth all the day long is the praise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is a sort of literature of the Jews: for to them let us refer this; there we shall find what hath been said: just as when we were inquiring about traders, on the score of actions and works, we found that to be called detestable trading, which the Apostle hath branded, saying, “For being ignorant of God’s righteousness, and willing to establish their own, to the righteousness of God they were not made subject.”…Just as then we found out the former charge against traders, that is men boasting of action, exalting themselves because of business which admitteth no inaction, unquiet men rather than good workmen; because good workmen are those in whom God worketh; so also we find a sort of literature among the Jews.…Moses wrote five books: but in the five porches encircling the pool, sick men were lying, but they could not be healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See how the letter remained, convicting the guilty, not saving the unrighteous.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in those five porches, a figure of the five books, sick men were given over rather than made whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then in that place did make whole a sick man?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The moving of the water.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When that pool was moved there went down a sick man, and there was made whole one, one because of unity: whatsoever other man went down unto that same moving was not made whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then was there commended the unity of the Body crying from the ends of the earth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another man was not healed, except again the pool were moved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The moving of the pool then did signify the perturbation of the people of the Jews when the Lord Jesus Christ came.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For at the coming of an Angel the water in the pool was perceived to be moved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The water then encircled with five porches was the Jewish nation encircled by the Law.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And in the porches the sick lay, and in the water alone when troubled and moved they were healed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Lord came, troubled was the water; He was crucified, may He come down in order that the sick man may be made whole.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, may He come down?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May He humble Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore whosoever ye be that love the letter without grace, in the porches ye will remain, sick ye will be, lying ill, not growing well.…For the same figure also it is that Eliseus at first sent a staff by his servant to raise up the dead child.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There had died the son of a widow his hostess; it was reported to him, to his servant he gave his staff: go thou, he saith, lay it on the dead child.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did the prophet not know what he was doing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The servant went before, he laid the staff upon the dead, the dead arose not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For if there had been given a law which could have made alive, surely out of the law there had been righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The law sent by the servant made not alive: and yet he sent his staff by the servant, who himself afterwards followed, and made alive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when that infant arose not, Eliseus came himself, now bearing the type of the Lord, who had sent before his servant with the staff, as though with the Law: he came to the child that was lying dead, he laid his limbs upon it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The one was an infant, the other a grown man: he contracted and shortened in a manner the size of his full growth, in order that he might fit the dead child.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The dead then arose, when he being alive adapted himself to the dead: and the Master did that which the staff did not; and grace did that which the letter did not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They then that have remained in the staff, glory in the letter; and therefore are not made alive.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I will to glory concerning Thy grace.…In that same grace I glorying “literature have not known:” that is, men on the letter relying, and from grace recoiling, with whole heart I have rejected.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: With reason there followeth, “I will enter into the power of the Lord:” not mine own, but the Lord’s.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they gloried in their own power of the letter, therefore grace joined to the letter they knew not.…But because “the letter killeth, but the Spirit maketh alive:” “I have not known literature, and I will enter into the power of the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore this verse following doth strengthen and perfect the sense, so as to fix it in the hearts of men, and not suffer any other interpretation to steal in from any quarter.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O Lord, I will be mindful of Thy righteousness alone” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ah! “alone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why hath he added “alone,” I ask you?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It would suffice to say, “I will be mindful of Thy righteousness.” “alone,” he saith, entirely: there of mine own I think not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For what hast thou which thou hast not received?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if also thou hast received, why dost thou glory as if thou hast not received.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thy righteousness alone doth deliver me, what is mine own alone is nought but sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May I not glory then of my own strength, may I not remain in the letter; may I reject “literature,” that is, men glorying of the letter, and on their own strength perversely, like men frantic, relying: may I reject such men, may I enter into the power of the Lord, so that when I am weak, then I may be mighty; in order that Thou in me mayest be mighty, for, “I will be mindful of Thy righteousness alone.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, Thou hast taught me from my youth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What hast thou taught me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That of Thy righteousness alone I ought to be mindful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For reviewing my past life, I see what was owing to me, and what I have received instead of that which was owing to me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There was owing punishment, there hath been paid grace: there was owing hell, there hath been given life eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, Thou hast taught me from my youth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the very beginning of my faith, wherewith Thou hast renewed me, Thou didst teach me that nothing had preceded in me, whence I might say that there was owing to me what Thou hast given.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who is turned to God save from iniquity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is redeemed save from captivity?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who can say that unjust was his captivity, when he forsook his Captain and fell off to the deserter?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God is for our Captain, the devil a deserter: the Captain gave a commandment, the deserter suggested guile: where were thine ears between precept and deceit? was the devil better than God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Better he that revolted than He that made thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst believe what the devil promised, and didst find what God threatened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now then out of captivity being delivered, still however in hope, not yet in substance, walking by faith, not yet by sight, “O God,” he saith, “Thou hast taught me from my youth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the time that I have been turned to Thee, renewed by Thee who had been made by Thee, re-created who had been created, re-formed who had been formed: from the time that I have been converted, I have learned that no merits of mine have preceded, but that Thy grace hath come to me gratis, in order that I might be mindful of Thy righteousness alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What next after youth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For, “Thou hast taught me,” he saith, “from my youth:” what after youth?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in that same first conversion of thine thou didst learn, how before conversion thou wast not just, but iniquity preceded, in order that iniquity being banished, there might succeed love: and having been renewed into a new man, only in hope, not yet in substance, thou didst learn how nothing of thy good had preceded, and by the grace of God thou wast converted to God: now perchance since the time that thou hast been converted wilt thou have anything of thine own, and on thy own strength oughtest thou to rely?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Just as men are wont to say, now leave me, it was necessary for thee to show me the way; it is sufficient, I will walk in the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And he that hath shown thee the way, “wilt thou not that I conduct thee to the place?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou, if thou art conceited, “let me alone, it is enough, I will walk in the way.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art left, and through thy weakness again thou wilt lose the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Good were it for thee that He should have conducted thee, who first put thee in the way.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But unless He too lead thee, again also thou wilt stray: say to Him then, “Conduct me, O Lord, in Thy way, and I will walk in Thy truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thy having entered on the way, is youth, the very renewal and beginning of the faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For before thou wast walking through thy own ways a vagabond; straying through woody places, through rough places, torn in all thy limbs, thou wast seeking a home, that is, a sort of settlement of thy spirit, where thou mightest say, it is well; and being in security mightest say it, at rest from every uneasiness, from every trial, in a word from every captivity; and thou didst not find.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What shall I say?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Came there to thee one to show thee the way?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There came to thee the Way itself, and thou wast set therein by no merits of thine preceding, for evidently thou wast straying.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What, since the time that thou hast set foot therein dost thou now direct thyself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth He that hath taught thee the way now leave thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: No, he saith: “Thou hast taught me from my youth; and even until now I will tell forth Thy wonderful works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a wonderful thing is that which still Thou doest; namely, that Thou dost direct me, who in the way hast put me: and these are Thy wonderful works.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What dost thou think to be the wonderful works of God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is more wonderful among God’s wonderful works, than the raising the dead?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But am I by any means dead, thou sayest?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Unless dead thou hadst been, there would not have been said to thee, “Rise, thou that sleepest, and arise from the dead, and Christ shall enlighten thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Dead are all unbelievers, all unrighteous men; in body they live, but in heart they are extinct.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he that raiseth a man dead according to the body, doth bring him back to see this light and to breathe this air: but he that raiseth is not himself light and air to him; he beginneth to see, as he saw before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A soul is not so resuscitated.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For a soul is resuscitated by God; though even a body is resuscitated by God: but God, when He doth resuscitate a body, to the world doth bring it back: when He doth resuscitate a soul, to Himself He bringeth it back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If the air of this world be withdrawn, there dieth body: if God be withdrawn, there dieth soul.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then God doth resuscitate a soul, unless there be with her He that hath resuscitated, she being resuscitated liveth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He doth not resuscitate, and then leave her to live to herself: in the same manner as Lazarus, when he was resuscitated after being four days dead, was resuscitated by the Lord’s corporal presence.…The Lord withdrew from that same city or from that spot, did Lazarus cease to live?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not so is the soul resuscitated: God doth resuscitate her, she dieth if God shall have withdrawn.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I will speak boldly, brethren, but yet the truth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Two lives there are, one of the body, another of the soul: as the life of the body is the soul, so the life of the soul is God: in like manner as, if the soul forsake, the body dieth: so the soul dieth, if God forsake.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then is His grace, namely, that He resuscitate and be with us.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because then He doth resuscitate us from our past death, and doth renew in a manner our life, we say to Him, “O God, Thou hast taught me from my youth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because He doth not withdraw from those whom He resuscitateth, lest when He shall have withdrawn from them they die, we say to Him, “and even until now I will tell forth Thy wonderful works:” because while Thou art with me I live, and of my soul Thou art the life, which will die if she be left to herself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore while my life is present, that is, my God, “even until now,” what next?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And even unto oldness and old age” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These are two terms for old age, and are distinguished by the Greeks.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the gravity succeeding youth hath another name among the Greeks, and after that same gravity the last age coming on hath another name; for πρεσβύτης signifieth grave, and γ™ρων old.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because in the Latin language the distinction of these two terms holdeth not, both words implying old age are inserted, oldness and old age: but ye know them to be two ages.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast taught me Thy grace from my youth; and even until now;” after my youth, “I will tell forth Thy wonderful works,” because Thou art with me in order that I may not die, who hast come in order that I may rise: “and even unto oldness and old age,” that is, even unto my last breath, unless with me Thou shalt have been, there will not be any merit of mine; may Thy grace alway remain with me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even one man would say this, thou, he, I; but because this voice is that of a certain great Man, that is, of the Unity itself, for it is the voice of the Church; let us investigate the youth of the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When Christ came, He was crucified, dead, rose again, called the Gentiles, they began to be converted, became Martyrs strong in Christ, there was shed faithful blood, there arose a harvest for the Church: this is Her youth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But seasons advancing let the Church confess, let Her say, “Even until now I will tell forth Thy wonderful works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not only in youth, when Paul, when Peter, when the first Apostles told: even in advancing age I myself, that is, Thy Unity, Thy members, Thy Body, “will tell forth Thy marvellous works.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And even unto oldness and old age,” I will tell forth Thy wonderful works: even until the end of the world here shall be the Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For if She were not to be here even unto the end of the world; to whom did the Lord say, “Behold, I am with you always, even unto the consummation of the world”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why was it necessary that these things should be spoken in the Scriptures?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because there were to be enemies of the Christian Faith who would say, “for a short time are the Christians, hereafter they shall perish, and there shall come back idols, there shall come back that which was before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How long shall be the Christians?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even unto oldness and old age:” that is, even unto the end of the world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When thou, miserable unbeliever, dost expect Christians to pass away, thou art passing away thyself without Christians: and Christians even unto the end of the world shall endure; and as for thee with thine unbelief when thou shalt have ended thy short life, with what face wilt thou come forth to the Judge, whom while thou wast living thou didst blaspheme?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “from my youth, and even until now, and even unto oldness and old age, O Lord, forsake not me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It will not be, as mine enemies say, even for a time.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Forsake not me, until I tell forth Thine arm to every generation that is yet to come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Arm of the Lord hath been revealed to whom?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Arm of the Lord is Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not Thou then forsake me: let not them rejoice that say, “only for a set time the Christians are.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May there be persons to tell forth Thine arm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To every generation that is yet to come.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then it be to every generation that is yet to come, it will be even unto the end of the world: for when the world is ended, no longer any generation will come on.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy power and Thy righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That is, that I may tell forth to every generation that is yet to come, Thine arm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what hath Thine arm effected?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This then let me tell forth, that same grace to every generation succeeding: let me say to every man that is to be born, nothing thou art by thyself, on God call thou, thine own are sins, merits are God’s: punishment to thee is owing, and when reward shall have come, His own gifts He will crown, not thy merits.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me say to every generation that is to come, out of captivity thou hast come, unto Adam thou didst belong.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me say this to every generation that is to come, that there is no strength of mine, no righteousness of mine; but “Thy strength and Thy righteousness, O God, even unto the most high mighty works which Thou hast made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy power and Thy righteousness,” as far as what? even unto flesh and blood?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nay, “even unto the most high mighty works which Thou hast made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the high places are the heavens, in the high places are the Angels, Thrones, Dominions, Principalities, Powers: to Thee they owe it that they are; to Thee they owe it that they live, to Thee they owe it that righteously they live, to Thee they owe it that blessedly they live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thy power and Thy righteousness,” as far as what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Even unto the most high mighty works which Thou hast made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not that man alone belongeth to the grace of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was Angel before he was made?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is Angel, if He forsake him who hath created?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore “Thy power and Thy justice even unto the most high mighty works which Thou hast made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And man exalteth himself: and in order that he may belong to the first captivity, he heareth the serpent suggesting, “Taste, and ye shall be as Gods.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Men as Gods?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, who is like unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not any in the pit, not in Hell, not in earth, not in Heaven, for all things Thou hast made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why doth the work strive with the Maker?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, who is like unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But as for me, saith miserable Adam, and Adam is every man, while I perversely will to be like unto Thee, behold what I have become, so that from captivity to Thee I cry out: I with whom it was well under a good king, have been made captive under my seducer; and cry out to Thee, because I have fallen from Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And whence have I fallen from Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While I perversely seek to be like unto Thee.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ill straying, ill presuming, doomed to die by withdrawing from the path of righteousness: behold he breaketh the commandment, he hath shaken off from his neck the yoke of discipline, uplifted with high spirit he hath broken in sunder the reins of guidance: where is he now?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly captive he crieth, “O Lord, who is like unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I perversely willed to be like unto Thee, and I have been made like unto a beast!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Under Thy dominion, under Thy commandment, I was indeed like: “But a man in honour set hath not perceived, he hath been compared to beasts without sense, and hath been made like unto them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now out of the likeness of beasts cry though late and say, “O God, who is like unto Thee?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “How great troubles hast Thou shown to me, many and evil!” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Deservedly, proud servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For thou hast willed perversely to be like thy God, who hadst been made after the image of thy Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wouldest thou have it to be well with thee, when withdrawing from that good?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Truly God saith to thee, if thou withdrawest from Me, and it is well with thee, I am not thy good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, if He is good, and in the highest degree good, and of Himself to Himself good, and by no foreign good thing good, and is Himself our chief good; by withdrawing from Him, what wilt thou be but evil?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Also if He is Himself our blessedness, what will there be to one withdrawing from Him, except misery?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Return thou then after misery, and say, “O Lord, who is like unto Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great troubles hast Thou shown to me, many and evil!”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But this was discipline; admonition, not desertion.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lastly, giving thanks, he saith what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And being turned Thou hast made me alive, and from the bottomless places of the earth again Thou hast brought me back.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when before?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this “again”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast fallen from a high place, O man, disobedient slave, O thou proud against thy Lord, thou hast fallen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There hast come to pass in thee, “every one that exalteth himself shall be humbled:” may there come to pass in thee, “every one that humbleth himself shall be exalted.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
