verses.text: Return thou from the deep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I return, he saith, I return, I acknowledge; “O God, who is like unto Thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great troubles hast Thou shown to me, many and evil! and being turned Thou hast made me alive, and from the bottomless places of the earth again Thou hast brought me back.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “We perceive,” I hear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast brought us back from the bottomless places of the earth, hast brought us back from the depth and drowning of sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But why “again”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When had it already been done?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us go on, if perchance the latter parts of the Psalm itself do not explain to us the thing which here we do not yet perceive, namely, why he hath said “again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore let us hear: “How great troubles Thou hast shown to me, many and evil!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And being turned Thou hast made me alive, and from the bottomless places of the earth again Thou hast brought me back.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast multiplied Thy righteousness, and being turned Thou hast comforted me, and from the bottomless places of the earth again Thou hast brought me back” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold a second “again”!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we labour to unravel this “again” when written once, who will be able to unravel it when doubled?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now “again” itself is a redoubling, and once more there is written “again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May He be with us from whom is grace, may there be with us the arm also which we are telling forth to every generation that is to come: may He be with us Himself, and as with the key of His Cross open to us the mystery that is locked up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it was not to no purpose that when He was crucified the veil of the temple was rent in the midst, but to show that through His Passion the secret things of all mysteries were opened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May He then Himself be with men passing over unto Him, be the veil taken away: may our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ tell us why such a voice of the Prophet hath been sent before, “Thou hast shown to me troubles many and evil: and being turned Thou hast made me alive, and from the bottomless places of the earth again Thou hast brought me back.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold this is the first “again” which hath been written.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us see what this is, and we shall see why there is a second “again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 27. …Therein Christ died, wherein thou art to die: and therein Christ rose again, wherein thou art to rise again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By His example He taught thee what thou shouldest not fear, for what thou shouldest hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst fear death, He died: thou didst despair of rising again, He rose again.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But thou sayest to me, He rose again, do I by any means rise again?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He rose again in that which for thee He received of thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore thy nature in Him hath preceded thee; and that which was taken of thee, hath gone up before thee: therein therefore thou also hast ascended.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore He ascended first, and we in Him: because that flesh is of the human race.…Behold one “again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear of its being fulfilled from the Apostle: “If then ye have risen with Christ, the things which are above seek ye, where Christ is sitting on the right hand of God; the things which are above mind ye, not the things which are upon the earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He then hath gone before: already we also have risen again, but still in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle Paul saying this same thing: “Even we ourselves groan in ourselves, looking for the adoption, the redemption of our body.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it then that Christ hath granted to thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hear that which followeth: “For by hope we are saved: but hope which is seen is not hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that which a man seeth, why doth he hope for?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But if that which we see not we hope for, through patience we wait for it.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have been brought back therefore again from the bottomless places in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because already Christ had gone before.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But because we shall rise again in substance, for now in hope we are living, now after faith we are walking; we have been brought back from the bottomless places of the earth, by believing in Him who before us hath risen again from the bottomless place of the earth.…Thou hast heard one “again,” thou hast heard the other: “again;” one “again” because of Christ going before; and the other, yet however in hope, and a thing which remaineth to be in substance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou hast multiplied Thy righteousness,” already in me believing, already in those that first have risen again in hope.…“Thou hast multiplied Thy righteousness, and being turned Thou hast comforted me:” and because of the body to rise again at the end, even from the bottomless places of the earth again Thou hast brought me back.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For I will confess to Thee in the vessels of a Psalm Thy truth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The vessels of a Psalm are a Psaltery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what is a Psaltery?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: An instrument of wood and strings.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth it signify?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There is some difference between it and a harp:…there seemeth to be signified by the Psaltery the Spirit, by the harp the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And because he had spoken of two bringings back of ours from the bottomless places of the earth, one after the Spirit in hope, the other after the body in substance; hear thou of these two: “For I will confess to Thee in the vessels of a Psalm Thy truth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This after the Spirit: concerning the body what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will psalm to Thee on a harp, Holy One of Israel.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again hear this because of that same “again” and “again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My lips shall exult when I shall psalm to Thee” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because lips are wont to be spoken of both belonging to the inner and to the outward man, it is uncertain in what sense lips have been used: there followeth therefore, “And my soul which Thou hast redeemed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore regarding the inward lips having been saved in hope, brought back from the bottomless places of the earth in faith and love, still however waiting for the redemption of our body, we say what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Already he hath said, “And my soul which Thou hast redeemed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But lest thou shouldest think the soul alone redeemed, wherein now thou hast heard one “again,” “but still,” he saith; why still? “but still my tongue also:” therefore now the tongue of the body: “all day long shall meditate of Thy righteousness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 24): that is, in eternity without end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when shall this be?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hereafter at the end of the world, at the resurrection of the body and the changing into the Angelic state.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence is it proved that this is spoken of the end, “but still my tongue also all day long shall meditate of Thy righteousness”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “When they shall have been confounded and shall have blushed, that seek evil things for me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When shall they be confounded, when shall they blush, save at the end of the world?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in two ways they shall be confounded, either when they shall believe in Christ, or when Christ shall have come.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so long as the Church is here, so long as grain groaneth amid chaff, so long as wheat groaneth amid tares, so long as vessels of mercy groan amid vessels of wrath made for dishonour, so long as lily groaneth amid thorns, there will not be wanting enemies to say, “When shall he die, and his name perish?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold there shall come the time when Christians shall be ended and shall be no more: as they began at a set time, so even unto a particular time they shall be.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But while they are saying these things and without end are dying, and while the Church is continuing preaching the Arm of the Lord to every generation that is to come; there shall come Himself also at last in His glory, there shall rise again all the dead, each with his cause: there shall be severed good men to the right hand, but evil men to the left, and they shall be confounded that did insult, they shall blush that did mock: and so my tongue after resurrection shall meditate of Thy righteousness, all day long of Thy praise, “when they shall have been confounded and shall have blushed, that seek evil things for me.”
